text:
Once upon a time in the serene forest of Eldergrove, there was a peculiar shop that existed unnoticed until its owner decided to make it known. This shop was run by an elderly woman named Martha, who had lost everything except her vast collection of antique porcelain rats.

Every morning, before dawn, Martha would wake up to the chirping of birds and the rustle of leaves. Her shop wasn't just any place; it was a haven for oddities. Martha wouldn't sell these fascinating creatures unless they fit a unique criteria—a rat just had to have a tiny pink bow on its hat.

One particular morning, the air hung with the sweetness of spring flowers. A young girl named Lily stumbled upon Martha's shop while searching for a new vase. Martha beckoned her inside.

"Why so eager?" Martha asked.

"Lately, I've lost everything I've ever loved."

"Then you've found a friend."

Lily picked up a tiny rat named Mr. Flutes—the hat was made
